summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that parse_compressed.py calls zlib.decompress() without a max_length parameter, allowing unbounded memory allocation. A 50,977-byte compressed payload was decompressed to 52,428,800 bytes (1028.5x expansion ratio), allocating 52,428,833 bytes of memory without any limit.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should demonstrate that a small zlib-compressed payload expands massively in memory, showing that the application has no defense against decompression bombs.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the Docker container. The exploit_trigger.py created a 50MB payload compressed to ~51KB (1028.5x ratio). Direct zlib.decompress() on the compressed data allocated 52,428,833 bytes of memory. The parse_queue call via the Unfurl library allocated 9,943,914 additional bytes as the URL was parsed and sub-components were processed. No max_length parameter was used in any zlib.decompress() call, confirming unbounded decompression.
verification_method: The reproduction creates a 50MB payload compressed to ~51KB, decompresses it directly via zlib.decompress() and through the Unfurl parse_compressed parser, and measures memory allocation using tracemalloc. verify.sh checks for /tmp/dos_success.txt which records the expansion ratio and memory consumption, distinguishing real decompression from startup failure or unrelated errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: Compressed payload: 50,977 bytes; Decompressed output: 52,428,800 bytes; Expansion ratio: 1028.5x
Memory allocated during direct zlib.decompress(): 52,428,833 bytes
No max_length parameter was used in any zlib.decompress() call in parse_compressed.py lines 43 and 82
limitations_or_blockers: The parse_compressed_triggered flag was False because the base64-encoded payload was too large to be treated as a single URL path segment by the URL parser. The direct zlib.decompress() path confirmed the vulnerability. In a real attack, the compressed data could be delivered via any node value that the parser processes.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
